notes.anchor: Asinius Pollio has written to me about that impure kinsman of ours
notes.note: C. Asinius Pollio, the soldier, orator, and historian, was at this date a Caesarian officer; here he relays unwelcome news about a disreputable relative of Cicero's. The veiled "impure kinsman" is unnamed in the letter and almost certainly Cicero's nephew Quintus, whose conduct toward Caesar had alarmed the family.

notes.anchor: Balbus the younger, recently, said it plainly enough
notes.note: L. Cornelius Balbus the Younger, nephew of Caesar's confidant Balbus the elder, was himself a Caesarian agent; he and the others had each reported on the kinsman's misconduct. He is distinct from his more famous uncle, who appears elsewhere in the correspondence simply as "Balbus."
